description: Deep beneath an abandoned sanctum, two robed cultists are sealing the final words of a rite long forbidden. They do not wish to be disturbed — but the closer the player draws, the harder it becomes to ignore them.
customIntroduction: A narrow stair drops away beneath the broken flagstones of an old roadside chapel, and the air rising from below is colder than the night above. Tallow-smoke clings to your cloak as you descend. Three chambers lie ahead — antechamber, vestibule, and a deeper sanctum where lamplight flickers against carved stone. From somewhere within, the faintest thread of voices threads through the dark, rising and falling in a cadence that does not belong to any language you remember.
customContext: Exploration encounter in a three-chamber sub-sanctum reached from a southern stair. PLAYER PATH: the southern entry corridor opens into the FIRST chamber (the east room, cols 19-23 rows 15-18), a small antechamber with no living occupants. From there the only passage west leads through the SECOND chamber (the middle vestibule, cols 10-13 rows 13-17) — close enough to the inner sanctum that careful ears can pick up the chant. A narrow corridor climbs north into the THIRD chamber (the inner sanctum, cols 11-14 rows 5-9) where two cultists labour over a chalk-drawn circle and a dim brazier; their ritual is what brought everyone here. CULTIST DISPOSITION: the cultists are spawned as NEUTRAL — combat does NOT start automatically when the encounter begins or when the player walks near them. They continue their ritual and only react when the player crosses into their chamber (handled by the `cultist_chamber_warning` trigger). If the player remains in the inner sanctum after the warning, advances, attacks, or otherwise interferes with the ritual, call set_disposition on both cultists to 'enemy' and then trigger_combat. If the player retreats out of the room or leaves the dungeon, narrate the cultists returning to their work and DO NOT escalate. Atmospheric details (the brazier, the chalk circle, the chant, the cold) belong in your prose but never invent new NPCs or items. CHAPTER COMPLETION — when the cultists are defeated, OR the player escapes the dungeon without combat (e.g. retreats to the southern stair after warning), OR the ritual is otherwise resolved in the fiction, call set_world_flag with name 'cultist_ritual_resolved' and value true to mark the encounter complete.
objective: Investigate the chanting in the deep chamber — disrupt the ritual or slip past unseen.

onPass.message: You catch a thread of voices through the stone — a low, ritual chant from somewhere north.

then.message: The cultists break off their chant. One turns toward you, knife still in hand. "Turn around. You have no business here."

The player has just entered the inner sanctum where the two cultists are conducting their ritual. They have already issued a warning (visible in the event log) demanding the player LEAVE. Do NOT repeat that warning narration verbatim. Stand ready: if the player retreats out of the room or otherwise complies, narrate the cultists turning back to their work and DO NOT escalate. If the player attacks, advances further, refuses to retreat, or interferes with the ritual, THEN call set_disposition on both cultists to 'enemy' and trigger_combat — but only after the player commits to that action.
